name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BACKLOG-15
title: Housekeeping wave 1: add Read-before-Edit warning to subagent prompt + simplify _close_call plumbing + test naming hygiene
description:
Bundled housekeeping items for tdd-subagent (companion to BUG-57/BUG-58 in the same cycle).

LOW (still worth doing in same cycle):
(F3) Assembled subagent prompt STEP 6 IMPLEMENT does not warn about the Edit-without-Read tool-state misfire pattern that BUG-6/Inv 35 addressed for the main session. A dispatched subagent Editing without prior Read will hit the same harness guard. Add 1-line warning to STEP 6 (or to agents/tdd-subagent.md). Cheap insurance against repeating the pattern in subagent context.

(F4) dispatch-tdd-subagent.py:238-312 — _close_call + _derive_feature_and_id + three parallel accumulator lists (close_call_lines + handoff_closed_items_lines + handoff_closed_items_json_entries) + YAML/JSON dual block construction = ~75 lines for 'render N item-status.py invocations and list them in two HANDOFF blocks'. Coding-rules section 2 'if 200 lines could be 50, rewrite' applies. Refactor: single loop building list of {feat, typ, iid, role} dicts + single template render. Cut ~50%.

(F5) test/test-backlog-4-6-7-9-10.py batched filename couples 4 unrelated backlog items into one file. Per-invariant or per-bug test files are the norm (test-bug-54-*, test-bypass-marker-*). When one backlog later closes or extends, the batched name loses meaning. Split into 4 per-backlog files.

Acceptance: STEP 6 IMPLEMENT in dispatch-tdd-subagent.py warns about Read-before-Edit; _close_call plumbing simplified; batched test file split.

NOTE: this backlog accompanies BUG-57 + BUG-58 in the tdd-subagent cycle, which itself depends on the contract cycle landing first (the new rabbit_print wrapper needed for BUG-57).

history.note: TDD cycle complete (secondary item resolved by same commit)
